name: vopono
title: vopono

commands.name: vopono

arguments.description: Suppress all output including application output
arguments.raw: --silent   Suppress all output including application output

arguments.description: read sudo password from program specified in SUDO_ASKPASS environment variable
arguments.raw: -A, --askpass  read sudo password from program specified in SUDO_ASKPASS environment variable

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# vopono -h
 Launch applications in a temporary VPN network namespace
 
 Usage: vopono [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]
 
 Commands:
 daemon   Run the vopono daemon (requires root)
 exec     Execute an application with the given VPN connection
 list     List running vopono namespaces and applications
 sync     Synchronise local server lists with VPN providers
 servers  List possible server configs for VPN provider, beginning with prefix
 help     Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
 
 Options:
 -v, --verbose  Verbose output
     --silent   Suppress all output including application output
 -A, --askpass  read sudo password from program specified in SUDO_ASKPASS environment variable
 -h, --help     Print help
 -V, --version  Print version

binary_metadata.file_type: ELF 64-bit LSB pie executable, x86-64, version 1 (SYSV), dynamically linked, interpreter /lib64/ld-linux-x86-64.so.2, BuildID[sha1]=b84e2e9441f7d223b42e3f7290047adb9675750d, for GNU/Linux 3.2.0, stripped

apt_metadata.description:
Run applications through VPN tunnels with temporary network namespaces
vopono is a tool to run applications through VPN tunnels via temporary
network namespaces. This allows you to run only a handful of applications
through different VPNs simultaneously, whilst keeping your main connection as
normal.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use vopono because ### vopono.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `vopono [OPTIONS] [COMMAND]` as documented upstream for vopono.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair vopono with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `vopono --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### vopono.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install vopono
